mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals a labor market inflection that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where demand is shifting from goods to services, causing earnings revisions downward and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint cross-sectional rank correlation of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of cooling housing starts on corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, HOUST declines do not produce statistically significant negative 20-day forward returns (p > 0.10) relative to periods with stable housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The sign of monthly PAYEMS changes fails to predict the sign of 5-day forward SPX returns with accuracy greater than 55% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate, it signals a demand-side slowdown that triggers downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below the 2.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of elevated federal funds rates and rising unemployment creates a wage-price spiral risk that forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy longer than expected, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE fails to explain more than 2% of the cross-sectional variance in 20-day forward SPX returns over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPI falls below 2.5%, a contraction in industrial production removes supply-side constraints on corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL inflation is below 2.5%, declines in INDPRO do not produce statistically significant positive 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) relative to periods of stable industrial output.
